---
title: TOPMed
alias: &ALIAS topmed
href: https://topmed.nhlbi.nih.gov/
listing:
  id: pubs
  template: ../../../../_ejs/publications-people.ejs 
  contents: 
    - "../../../../publications/**/*.qmd"
    - "!../../../../publications/_template/"
  sort: "pub_number desc"
  include:
    categories: *ALIAS
  fields: [publication, title, categories, image, date, author]

---

## Overview

The Trans-Omics for Precision Medicine (TOPMed) program is an NHLBI initiative integrating whole-genome sequencing with other molecular data and phenotypic information to improve precision medicine for heart, lung, blood, and sleep disorders.

## Key Features

- Whole-genome sequenced over 180,000 individuals from diverse ancestral backgrounds
- Integrates WGS with multi-omic data including methylation, metabolomics, and proteomics
- Provides controlled-access data via dbGaP, with summary statistics publicly available
- Identifies rare and common variants associated with complex disease phenotypes
- Employs harmonized phenotype data across participating cohorts for meta-analysis

## Use Cases in Research

- Identifying genetic variants associated with susceptibility to respiratory or infectious diseases
- Using population-scale WGS data to contextualize rare variants in AMR-relevant immune genes
- Accessing multi-ethnic variant frequency data for pharmacogenomics analyses
- Providing genetic reference panels for imputation in smaller infectious disease cohort studies
- Modeling polygenic risk scores for immune-mediated traits intersecting with infection outcomes

## Getting Started

Visit [TOPMed](https://topmed.nhlbi.nih.gov/) to access the database.

## Related Publications

:::{#pubs}
:::
